children.heading: Procedural, investigative, and enforcement powers of Federal Trade Commission
children.label: a Procedural, investigative, and enforcement powers of Federal Trade Commission
children.text: The Federal Trade Commission shall have procedural, investigative, and enforcement powers, including the power to issue procedural rules in enforcing compliance with the requirements of this subchapter and rules prescribed pursuant to the requirements of this subchapter, to further define terms used in this subchapter, and to require the filing of reports, the production of documents, and the appearance of witnesses, as though the applicable terms and conditions of the Federal Trade Commission Act [ et seq.] were part of this subchapter.15 U.S.C. 41

children.heading: Testing, certification, and notice requirements of Environmental Protection Agency; interagency enforcement agreements between Federal Trade Commission and Environmental Protection Agency and other Federal agencies
children.label: b Testing, certification, and notice requirements of Environmental Protection Agency; interagency enforcement agreements between Federal Trade Commission and Environmental Protection Agency and other Federal agencies
A
children.text: may conduct field testing of the automotive fuel rating of automotive fuel, comparing the tested automotive fuel rating of fuel at retail outlets with the automotive fuel rating posted at those outlets;

children.label: B
children.text: shall certify the results of such tests and comparisons to the Federal Trade Commission; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall notify the Federal Trade Commission of any failure to post the automotive fuel rating.

children.label: A
children.text: minimizing the cost of field inspection and related compliance activities; and

children.label: B
children.text: reducing duplication of similar or related field compliance activities performed by agencies of the United States.

children.heading: Promulgation of rules by Federal Trade Commission; contents; requirements for compliance with rules
children.label: c Promulgation of rules by Federal Trade Commission; contents; requirements for compliance with rules
A
children.text: a uniform method by which a person may certify to another the automotive fuel rating of automotive fuel; and

children.label: B
children.text: a uniform method of displaying the automotive fuel rating of automotive fuel at the point of sale to ultimate purchasers.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be considered to satisfy the requirements of subsection (a) or (b) of , as the case may be, only if such person complies with the requirements established pursuant to paragraph (1)(A); andsection 2822 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: shall be considered to satisfy the requirements of only if such person complies with the requirements established pursuant to paragraph (1)(B).section 2822(c) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: ease of administration and enforcement, and

children.label: ii
children.text: industry practices in the distribution and marketing of automotive fuel; and

children.label: B
children.text: may permit adjustments in such automotive fuel rating to take into account the effects of altitude, temperature, and humidity.

The Federal Trade Commission may, by rule, prescribe and make effective a method of determining the automotive fuel rating of automotive fuel which consists of a blend of two or more quantities of automotive fuel of different automotive fuel ratings if the Federal Trade Commission finds that the method prescribed more accurately reflects the automotive fuel rating of such blend than the weighted-average method set forth in . Effective on and after the effective date of such rule, any person shall be considered to satisfy the requirements of only if such person utilizes the method prescribed in such rule (in lieu of the method set forth in ).section 2822(f)(1) of this titlesection 2822(f)(1) of this titlesection 2822(f)(1) of this title

children.heading: Statutory provisions applicable for promulgation of rules
children.label: d Statutory provisions applicable for promulgation of rules

children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), rules under this subchapter shall be prescribed in accordance with , except that interested persons shall be afforded an opportunity to present written and oral data, views, and arguments with respect to any proposed rule.section 553 of title 5
Rules prescribed under subsection (c)(3) and shall be prescribed on the record after opportunity for an agency hearing.section 2822(d) of this title
Section 18 of the Federal Trade Commission Act () shall not apply with respect to any rule prescribed under this subchapter.15 U.S.C. 57a

children.heading: Acts or practices constituting violations
children.label: e Acts or practices constituting violations
children.text: It shall be an unfair or deceptive act or practice in or affecting commerce (within the meaning of section 5(a)(1) of the Federal Trade Commission Act []) for any person to violate subsection (a), (b), (c), or (e) of , or a rule prescribed under subsection (d) of . For purposes of the Federal Trade Commission Act [ et seq.] (including any remedy or penalty applicable to any violation thereof) such a violation shall be treated as a violation of a rule under such Act respecting unfair or deceptive acts or practices.15 U.S.C. 45(a)(1)section 2822 of this titlesection 2822 of this title15 U.S.C. 41
